---
# Source Card: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

## Summary
KDIGO 2024 is the current authoritative international CKD guideline (Kidney Int vol 105, Suppl 4S), superseding the 2012 version. It establishes the CKD definition (kidney structure/function abnormalities present for >=3 months) and the CGA classification (Cause, GFR category G1-G5, Albuminuria category A1-A3). For the pediatric CDS module its load-bearing content sits in the "Pediatric considerations" practice points: children and adolescents over age 2 get a higher "low GFR" flag of <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 (vs the adult/2012 cutoff of 60), and eGFR in children should be computed with a pediatric-validated equation such as CKiD U25 2021, using creatinine assays QC'd for the pediatric low range.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) CKD = abnormalities present >=3 months — locator: p.21 "Defining CKD" — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0ea8b16cae112ae2408ec7befe4dc345919ee295cfe0ad43ccfa2c079f3e619c]"
- (ev_002) Pediatric 'low GFR' flag <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 for children/adolescents >2 y — locator: p.70 Practice Point 1.2.3.5 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cb4ad1345056f722749d7ff2582e97d350306ea069177b84501e6e90179fa1f9]"
- (ev_003) GFR categories G1 (>=90) to G5 (<15) — locator: p.10 CGA nomenclature — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:e199bf81167dc729fe0e928cc285432845b2994febfb1be6feef26e008eaaa9c]".
- (ev_004) Albuminuria A1 <30 / A2 30-300 / A3 >300 mg/g ACR — locator: p.10 CGA nomenclature — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:40f71e337cebe4ddc29438ac993138b573b1367408566d5a02e8c7cffc28b2f2]".
- (ev_005) Estimate pediatric GFR with a validated equation (CKiD U25 2021, ages 1-25; age/sex/height) — locator: p.74 Practice Point 1.2.4.3 + p.73 Table 14 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8ba4ee84957972bf8ae02e783eb0b0dce27f78a569499ea6c15ad11b27771bf7]".
- (ev_006) Enzymatic (not Jaffe) creatinine assay preferred in children; QC lowest range — locator: p.70 Practice Point 1.2.3.4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7b98c1e45ed4a755e4fe834c99a69ffa76c395e2dc9d03e559d66336afeae22f]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: guideline covers children and adults; the general CGA thresholds (ev_003, ev_004) are population-general staging, whereas the actionable pediatric-specific values are the <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 "low" flag (children/adolescents >2 y) and the CKiD U25 age band 1-25.
- Assay/method: pediatric eGFRcr is equation- and assay-dependent — KDIGO advises enzymatic creatinine and a pediatric-validated equation (CKiD U25 2021), and warns CKiD U25 performance is uncertain in the very young, very low GFR, or outside Europe/North America.
- Lifecycle: KDIGO 2024, effective 2024-04, supersedes KDIGO 2012. Key pediatric change vs 2012: the child "low GFR" flag rises from 60 to 90 mL/min/1.73 m2.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The pediatric <90 mL/min/1.73 m2 "low" flag functions as a monitoring/closer-assessment trigger, not a diagnosis: the guideline frames it as reflecting "the need for closer assessment for evidence of kidney damage and monitoring" and notes CKD G2 in children is associated with poor kidney outcomes. This is a candidate scope/referral signal for the CDS converter, not an executable directive. The CKD definition itself is unchanged; the flag is advisory.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP: the "GFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m2" CKD-defining decreased-GFR criterion lives in Table 1, which renders as a graphic in the PDF and was NOT locatable as extractable verbatim text; therefore it is not asserted here. The category boundaries in ev_003 (which encompass <60 across G3a-G5) are quoted instead from the CGA nomenclature figure.
- CONFLICT (preserved, not collapsed): the pediatric "low GFR" flag of <90 (ev_002) conflicts with (a) the adult context and (b) the KDIGO 2012 pediatric cutoff of 60 mL/min/1.73 m2, which KDIGO 2024 explicitly raises to 90. The CDS module must keep the adult vs pediatric thresholds distinct and must not apply the adult 60 cutoff to children >2 y.
- Population gaps: no separate pediatric ACR cutoffs are given (ev_004 categories are population-general); the <90 flag explicitly excludes children <=2 years, for whom no numeric flag is stated here (missingness — do not infer one); CKiD U25 (ev_005) is uncertain in the very young and very low GFR.
- This card is a PROPOSAL for the CDS converter, not an executable rule; no diagnosis, treatment, dosing, or transfusion directive is expressed.
